# pong-ball

> pong-ball is a free, open-source, minimalist Pong game that runs in the terminal. It uses sub-stepped collision physics (the ball never tunnels through walls or glitches), a spring-driven paddle controlled by keyboard or mouse, five restrained dark themes, four game modes, terminal-bell sound, and a persistent local score history. It is written in Go and ships as a single binary for macOS, Linux, and Windows.

## About

- Name: pong-ball
- Author: Subhadeep Roy (https://github.com/subhadeeproy3902)
- License: MIT (free and open source)
- Language: Go, built with the Bubble Tea TUI framework
- Website: https://pongball.mvp-subha.me
- Source: https://github.com/subhadeeproy3902/pong-ball

## Features

- Sub-stepped continuous collision physics — smooth and glitch-free at any speed
- Spring-driven paddle, controllable with arrow keys, A/D, or the mouse
- Five themes: Claude (default), Mono, Nord, Moss, Ember — restrained, single-accent palettes
- Four modes: Classic (one life), Arcade (lives + power-ups), Zen (endless), Time Trial (60s)
- Six power-ups and five escalating difficulty phases
- Persistent JSON score history with an in-game leaderboard
- Single static binary, zero runtime dependencies

## Install

- macOS / Linux one-liner: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/subhadeeproy3902/pong-ball/main/install.sh | bash`
- Homebrew: `brew install subhadeeproy3902/pong-ball/pong-ball`
- Go: `go install github.com/subhadeeproy3902/pong-ball@latest`
- Docker: `docker run --rm -it ghcr.io/subhadeeproy3902/pong-ball:latest`
- Binaries, .deb/.rpm/.apk packages, and Windows zip: https://github.com/subhadeeproy3902/pong-ball/releases

## Controls

- Arrow keys or A/D, or the mouse — move the paddle
- P or Space — pause; T — cycle theme; M — toggle sound; R — restart; Q — quit
